author: atacansuder
description: Fast, offline Japanese popup dictionary using Yomitan dictionaries. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Atacan Süder
manifest.description: Fast, offline Japanese popup dictionary using Yomitan dictionaries.
manifest.name: Japanese Popup Dictionary

name: Japanese Popup Dictionary

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Fixed furigana display for expressions where kana and kanji are mixed, avoiding one full reading over the entire expression.
 (Example: <ruby>食べる <rt>たべる</rt></ruby> → <ruby>食<rt>た</rt></ruby>べる)
- Fixed cells in form tables not being rendered properly where symbols for priority, rare, irregular, outdated, old, and valid forms were not shown.
- Added tooltips to symbols in form lists.
- Changed tooltips from `title` attribute to `aria-label` which increases accessibility and aligns with Obsidian's native tooltip implementation where aria-labels are also visually shown as tooltips.
- Reduced padding of ordered lists inside definitions for consistency and better readability.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Dictionary popup now repositions itself to stay within the viewport. Previously, hovering over Japanese text near any screen edge could cause the popup to be partially clipped. It now flips horizontally (left/right) and vertically (above/below) based on available space.
- Clicking dictionary links whose display text contains ruby tags (e.g. <ruby>約<rt>やく</rt></ruby>) no longer throws a TypeError. The base text is now correctly extracted from the ruby structure before lookup.
- Minor code cleanup

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Various code improvements for issues detected by Obsidian's automated checks
- Fixed a bug where scans would not work on notes opened in new windows
- Fixed a bug where scans would not work on Obsidian versions installed by older installers
- Added build provenance attestation
